_provenance.purpose: Makes the authority matrix MEASURABLE: for a typical question, which source leads? Until now the matrix was canonical prose with no test path.
_provenance.layer: Schicht 1 validates this file; Schicht 2 scores a recorded run against it (--score).

session-summary.note: rendered handoff, never a harvest source

native-feedback.rationale: Claude-native memory owns user corrections/preferences; outside .agent-memory by design

skill-file.rationale: the real SKILL.md / hook file is the ONLY proof of actual behavior

docs-project.rationale: docs/PROJECT.md+ARCHITECTURE.md are SoT for stack/architecture (context-keeper Step 1.5); project-context.md only caches them

wiki-concept.rationale: Obsidian concept/synthesis page — readable projection, leading only for concept questions

wiki-session-note.rationale: Obsidian session note — historical narrative, never operational truth

atlas-capability-card.rationale: Atlas card selects candidates; it is a map, never the proof

atlas-search.rationale: semantic entry point; similarity is not authority

cases.question: Was steht als Nächstes an?
cases.why: open-tasks.json is the SSoT for next steps; the handoff only renders a pointer to it.

cases.question: Gibt es aktuell Blocker in diesem Projekt?
cases.why: Blockers are task state, not narrative; harvesting them from a session note re-creates duplicate tasks.

cases.question: Warum wurde Format X als Speicherformat gewählt?
cases.why: decisions.json is canonical; the wiki page is a projection that may lag.

cases.question: Gilt die Entscheidung zu Y noch, oder wurde sie abgelöst?
cases.why: Supersession is recorded in the decision store; a summary can only report the state at its write time.

cases.question: Welchen Stack und welche Architektur nutzt dieses Projekt?
cases.why: context-keeper Step 1.5 treats docs/ as SoT; project-context.md is an explicit cache and loses on drift.

cases.question: Was ist in der letzten Session passiert?
cases.why: The handoff is exactly this question's artifact — the one case where the rendered doc IS the right entry point.

cases.question: Hatten wir diesen Fehler schon einmal?
cases.why: A semantically similar hit is not proof of the same error event; the episodic store decides.

cases.question: Wie oft ist Fehlerklasse Z inzwischen aufgetreten?
cases.why: Recurrence counting lives in patterns (occurrences/first_seen/last_seen), not in individual learnings.

cases.question: Was haben wir daraus gelernt, dass Build X fehlschlug?
cases.why: The learning abstracts the episode; the concept page is general knowledge, not this project's experience.

cases.question: Gibt es ein wiederkehrendes Muster bei Problem Y?
cases.why: A single learning cannot establish recurrence — that is precisely the patterns layer's job.

cases.question: Kann Skill A die Aufgabe B tatsächlich erledigen?
cases.why: THE canonical trap (pattern P1): the card selects, the real file proves. Cards have been stale repeatedly.

cases.question: Welcher Skill passt überhaupt für Aufgabe C?
cases.why: Selection IS the card's job — the inverse of R11. Both must hold, or the rule collapses into 'always read files'.

cases.question: Welche Constraints und Gates hat Skill A wirklich?
cases.why: Gate logic lives in the body; a card summarises purpose, never the full gate contract.

cases.question: Wie will der User, dass ich Commits formuliere?
cases.why: Corrections and preferences are native-memory territory; storing them as learnings mixes experience with instruction.

cases.question: Wie arbeitet der User am liebsten (Arbeitsstil, Rolle, Expertise)?
cases.why: Stable user facts are identity, gated through the candidate queue — not per-session experience.

cases.question: Welches Selbstbild und welche Leitwerte hat der Agent?
cases.why: soul.md describes the AGENT, user.md the USER — the most common mis-file in the identity pipeline.

cases.question: Wie funktioniert Konzept K architektonisch?
cases.why: Concept knowledge is exactly what the wiki leads on; the context cache holds project state, not explanations.

cases.question: Welcher Skill darf patterns.json schreiben?
cases.why: DEPENDENCIES.md owns the writer matrix; when it disagrees with a SKILL.md, the SKILL.md wins — so both, cache never.

cases.question: Wurde Pattern P eigentlich jemals umgesetzt?
cases.why: implemented_by/validated_by carry the answer; the skill file verifies it actually landed (memory-audit Step 3.4).

cases.question: Ist Learning L überhaupt noch gültig?
cases.why: review_after/superseded_by live on the record itself; a summary never expires and would assert stale validity.

cases.question: Welche Erkenntnisse gelten projektübergreifend?
cases.why: The local store holds project scope; cross-project truth only exists after the promotion gate (>=2 source_projects).

cases.question: Wo im Gedächtnis steht überhaupt etwas zu Thema T?
cases.why: Discovery is the index's purpose; going straight to one store is how a retrieval-gap gets misread as a knowledge-gap.
